# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';

export function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
  const authCookie = req.cookies.get('authToken');
  
  if (!authCookie) {
    return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
  }

  try {
    const { payload, signature } = parseJwt(authCookie);

    if (isValidToken(payload, signature)) {
      return NextResponse.next();
    } else {
      return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
    }
  } catch (e) {
    return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
  }
}

function parseJwt(token: string) {
  const [header, payload, signature] = token.split('.');
  if (!header || !payload || !signature) throw new Error('Invalid token format');
  return { payload: JSON.parse(atob(payload)), signature };
}

function isValidToken(payload: any, signature: string) {
  const now = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);
  return now < payload.exp && verifySignature(payload, signature);
}

function verifySignature(payload: any, signature: string) {
  // Implement your logic to verify the signature
  return true; // Placeholder for actual signature verification logic
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected-route', '/another-protected-route'], // Add your routes here
};
